stack.name: BPMN Engine
stack.description: BPMN engine with REST API. Design processes in the built-in modeler and deploy with one click.

stack.name: Connector Runtime
stack.description: Universal runtime with 22 connectors across 4 categories: AI, Communication, Productivity, and General.

stack.name: BPMN Modeler
stack.description: Design BPMN processes in the browser and deploy to Camunda with one click. No desktop app needed.

stack.name: Log Viewer
stack.description: Real-time Docker log viewer. Stream and filter logs from all containers in one place.

stack.name: Database Browser
stack.description: Browse and query your PostgreSQL database directly in the browser. Inspect tables, run SQL, export data.

stack.name: Container Manager
stack.description: Monitor and manage your Docker containers. Restart services, view resource usage, check health.

tips.title: Access all your services from one place
tips.text: Studio runs everything locally. The BPMN Engine, Log Viewer, Database Browser, Container Manager, and the BPMN Modeler are all accessible from the sidebar.

tips.title: Secret management built in
tips.text: Store API keys in environment variables, files, or properties — then reference them by name in your connector templates. No secrets in your BPMN files.

tips.title: One-click deploy
tips.text: Design your BPMN process in the modeler, click Deploy, and it's running on your local engine. No CI pipeline, no build step, no waiting.

items.text: Connector runtime settings now configurable via Docker Compose environment variables
Version number now displays correctly on the Studio home page
Updates page copy buttons work correctly in all browsers
Updates page theme follows Studio light/dark mode in real time
Reduced log noise in web modeler container
Light/dark mode toggle with theme selector
Updates page matches Studio light/dark theme automatically
Update commands split into separate steps with copy buttons
Automated minor version bump on every merge to main
Docker images now tagged with version number in addition to :latest
Operaton support — run Camunda 7 and Operaton side-by-side with engine switching
Plugin architecture for connector runtime
DMN support in web modeler with Save As and unsaved changes dialog
10 new AI connectors: Azure OpenAI, DeepSeek, Gemini, Groq, Mistral, Ollama, Perplexity, Together AI, Fireworks AI, OpenAI-Compatible
Jira connector suite — Create, Update, Comment, Transition with example BPMN
Microsoft Teams connector (Incoming Webhook)
SendGrid email connector
Slack native connector
Generic REST connector
Webhook connector for outbound events
File import in modeler with connector grouping and path hints
Resources page with FEEL reference links
Connector README rendering in Studio UI
Upgraded Camunda from 7.21.0 to 7.24.0
Changed Camunda host port to 8090 to avoid local conflicts
Lucide React icons replacing custom icons
Brand logos and wider connector cards
Simplified connector naming convention
Operaton iframe embedding (CSP headers)
PostgreSQL init error on fresh installs
Parameterised hardcoded URLs in example BPMNs
Jira suite navigation and rendering
Universal connector runtime with secret management (env, file, properties, variable)
AI connectors: Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI Grok
Built-in BPMN modeler with one-click deploy to Camunda
Docker Compose stack: Camunda 7, n8n, PostgreSQL, Caddy reverse proxy
Studio control panel with service dashboard

feedback.text: Catalyst Studio is brand new. Your feedback shapes what we build next.
